title: Thyroid nodule surgical clinic

medication.text: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.
medication.rationale: Supports rapid growth, hoarse voice, swallowing and breathing warning symptoms for thyroid nodule surgical clinic; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — information and thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms

referral.text: Supports clinician assessment of palpable thyroid enlargement or focal nodularity, recommendation-qualified ultrasound, compression symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.
referral.rationale: Supports palpable enlargement or focal nodularity, qualified ultrasound and compression context for thyroid nodule surgical clinic; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of palpable thyroid enlargement or focal nodularity, recommendation-qualified ultrasound, compression symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.8 — thyroid enlargement with normal thyroid function

referral.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.
referral.rationale: Supports clinician recognition of an unexplained thyroid lump without asserting cancer for thyroid nodule surgical clinic; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Nodule duration and growth, thyroid function, ultrasound risk features, cytology, molecular testing, diagnosis, malignancy risk, surveillance, biopsy, surgery, consent and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for thyroid nodule surgical clinic.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
